 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2my","title":"Integration Tests: Run Command (dry-run, render, preflight)","description":"# Task: Integration Tests for Run Command

## Objective
Test the run command comprehensively using dry-run and render modes (no actual Oracle calls).

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Dry Run Mode (--dry-run)
apr run 1 --dry-run
Verify output shows:
- Oracle command that would be executed
- Model selection
- File arguments
- Slug format
- Output file path
- All flags passed correctly

### 2. Render Mode (--render)
apr run 1 --render
Verify:
- Prompt content rendered to stdout
- Files would be included
- No Oracle process started

### 3. Render with Copy (--render --copy)
apr run 1 --render --copy
Verify clipboard integration (if available)

### 4. Preflight Validation
Test successful preflight: apr run 1 --dry-run
- Verify: All pre-flight checks passed
Test failed preflight (missing file):
- Verify: Error message, non-zero exit
Test skip preflight: apr run 1 --dry-run --no-preflight
- Verify: Skips checks, continues

### 5. Include Implementation Flag
apr run 1 --dry-run --include-impl
Verify:
- Implementation file in command args
- Slug includes -with-impl
- Correct template loaded

### 6. Workflow Selection
apr run 1 --dry-run -w myworkflow
apr run 1 --dry-run --workflow myworkflow
Verify correct workflow loaded

### 7. Output File Handling
Test existing output file:
- Verify: Warning about existing file
- Interactive: Prompt for overwrite
- Non-interactive: Proceeds

### 8. Round Number Validation
Invalid round numbers:
- apr run abc = Error: must be positive integer
- apr run -1 = Error: must be positive integer
- apr run 0 = Works (edge case)
- apr run 999 = Works
Shorthand: apr 5 equivalent to apr run 5

### 9. Verbose Mode
apr run 1 --dry-run --verbose
Verify verbose output includes:
- Config loading details
- Option parsing details
- File validation details

### 10. Quiet Mode
apr run 1 --dry-run --quiet
Verify minimal output (errors only)

### 11. run_oracle_with_retry() (NEW)
Test the Oracle execution wrapper:
- Retry logic on transient failures
- Heartbeat option passed
- Notify option passed
- Write-output option passed
- Background execution handling
- PID tracking for robot mode

### 12. Session Slug Generation (NEW)
Verify slug format:
- apr-{workflow}-round-{N}
- apr-{workflow}-round-{N}-with-impl (when --include-impl)
- Special characters handled in workflow name

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] dry-run shows complete Oracle command
- [ ] render outputs prompt correctly
- [ ] Preflight validation working
- [ ] --no-preflight skips checks
- [ ] --include-impl flag working
- [ ] Workflow selection working
- [ ] Round number validation correct
- [ ] Verbose/quiet modes working
- [ ] run_oracle_with_retry tested
- [ ] Session slug format verified
- [ ] All option combinations tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:10:13.95116383-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:18:49.279835875-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2my","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-uos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.166915418-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2my","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2uc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.19214779-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2my","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ifg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.217601861-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2my","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ixw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.243921532-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2uc","title":"Unit Tests: Preflight and Validation Functions","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Preflight and Validation Functions

## Objective
Test pre-run validation with REAL file system checks.

## Functions to Test

### 1. preflight_check() - Pre-flight Validation
```bash
# Test cases with real files:
# Setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/apr_test
echo "# README" > /tmp/apr_test/README.md
echo "# SPEC" > /tmp/apr_test/SPEC.md
echo "# IMPL" > /tmp/apr_test/IMPL.md

# Happy path:
- All required files exist → return 0
- Oracle available → success message

# File validation:
- README missing → return 1, error message
- README not readable (chmod 000) → return 1
- Spec missing → return 1
- Spec not readable → return 1

# Implementation file (optional):
- impl_path provided but missing → return 2 (warning)
- impl_path provided but not readable → return 2 (warning)
- impl_path provided and valid → return 0

# Oracle checks:
- Oracle not available → return 1
- Oracle version check fails → return 2 (warning)

# Output verification:
- File sizes displayed correctly
- Appropriate success/error/warning messages
```

### 2. run_round() Validation Path
```bash
# Test the validation within run_round:
- Missing workflow config → friendly welcome or error
- Missing required documents → error
- include_impl but no impl configured → warning
- include_impl but impl file missing → warning, continue without
- Output file exists → prompt for overwrite
```

### 3. Robot Mode Validation (robot_validate)
```bash
# Test cases:
- Round number missing → error JSON
- Not initialized → error JSON
- Workflow not found → error JSON
- README missing → errors array populated
- Spec missing → errors array populated
- Oracle not available → errors array populated
- Previous round missing → warnings array (not error)
- All valid → valid:true response
```

## Test Fixtures Required
```
tests/fixtures/documents/
├── valid_readme.md      # Well-formed README
├── valid_spec.md        # Well-formed spec
├── valid_impl.md        # Well-formed implementation
├── empty.md             # Empty file
└── binary_file          # Non-text file
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] preflight_check return codes correct
- [ ] All file checks use real filesystem
- [ ] Error messages helpful and specific
- [ ] Warning vs error distinction correct
- [ ] robot_validate JSON output valid
- [ ] Verbose logging for debugging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:09:15.467431863-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:45:13.912328618-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:45:13.912328618-05:00","close_reason":"Added unit tests for preflight/run_round validation/robot_validate and fixtures","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-2uc","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:12:37.357618897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-4nt","title":"Integration Tests: Setup Wizard and Workflow Creation","description":"# Task: Integration Tests for Setup Wizard

## Objective
Test the complete setup wizard flow with real file creation.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. First-Time Setup (Interactive)
```bash
# Use expect or similar for interactive testing
# Flow:
1. apr setup (with no existing .apr)
2. Enter workflow name
3. Select/enter README path
4. Select/enter spec path  
5. Optionally select implementation path
6. Select GPT model
7. Verify:
   - .apr/config.yaml created
   - .apr/workflows/<name>.yaml created
   - .apr/rounds/<name>/ directory created
   - Config contains correct paths
   - Default workflow set in config.yaml

# Automated version using predefined inputs:
echo -e "test-workflow\n\nREADME.md\nSPEC.md\n\n5.2 Thinking" | apr setup
```

### 2. Additional Workflow Setup
```bash
# With existing .apr directory:
1. apr setup
2. Create second workflow
3. Verify:
   - New workflow config created
   - Default workflow unchanged
   - Both workflows listed by apr list
```

### 3. Setup with Implementation Doc
```bash
# Flow including implementation:
1. apr setup
2. Provide all three document paths
3. Verify implementation path in config
```

### 4. Setup Validation
```bash
# Test validation during setup:
- Workflow name with spaces → handled
- Workflow name with special chars → handled or rejected
- Non-existent file paths → warning/error
- Empty workflow name → error
```

### 5. Gum vs ANSI Modes
```bash
# Test both UI modes:
APR_NO_GUM=1 apr setup  # Test ANSI fallback
apr setup               # Test gum mode (if available)
```

## Test Implementation
```bash
@test "setup creates correct directory structure" {
    cd "$TEST_DIR"
    mkdir project && cd project
    echo "# README" > README.md
    echo "# SPEC" > SPECIFICATION.md
    
    # Simulate interactive input
    run bash -c 'echo -e "myworkflow\n\nREADME.md\nSPECIFICATION.md\n\n5.2 Thinking" | apr setup'
    
    log_test_output "$output"
    
    assert_success
    assert [ -d ".apr" ]
    assert [ -f ".apr/config.yaml" ]
    assert [ -f ".apr/workflows/myworkflow.yaml" ]
    assert [ -d ".apr/rounds/myworkflow" ]
    
    # Verify config content
    assert grep -q "default_workflow: myworkflow" .apr/config.yaml
    assert grep -q "readme: README.md" .apr/workflows/myworkflow.yaml
}
```

## Test Fixtures
```
tests/fixtures/setup/
├── sample_readme.md    # Sample README for testing
├── sample_spec.md      # Sample specification
└── sample_impl.md      # Sample implementation
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Fresh setup creates all required files/dirs
- [ ] Additional workflow setup works
- [ ] Implementation doc optional and works
- [ ] Both gum and ANSI modes tested
- [ ] Validation catches invalid inputs
- [ ] Generated config is valid YAML
- [ ] Detailed logging of each step","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:09:53.762145144-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:39:56.994357099-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:39:56.994357099-05:00","close_reason":"Created tests/integration/test_setup.bats with 23 passing tests covering setup wizard flow, validation, UI modes, model selection, and edge cases","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-4nt","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-uos","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.109980513-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-4nt","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ifg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:50.137454549-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-8ij","title":"Unit Tests: XDG Compliance and Path Functions","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for XDG Compliance and Path Functions

## Objective
Test that APR correctly uses XDG Base Directory paths and path utility functions.

## Functions to Test

### 1. XDG Path Configuration
```bash
# APR should use:
# - Data: ${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/apr/
# - Cache: ${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/apr/
# - Config: Per-project .apr/ directory

# Test cases:
- XDG_DATA_HOME set → data goes there
- XDG_DATA_HOME unset → uses ~/.local/share/apr/
- XDG_CACHE_HOME set → cache goes there
- XDG_CACHE_HOME unset → uses ~/.cache/apr/

# Directory creation:
- Data directory created if missing
- Cache directory created if missing
- Proper permissions (700 or 755)
```

### 2. APR_HOME Variable
```bash
# Test cases:
- APR_HOME set → overrides default
- APR_HOME unset → uses XDG_DATA_HOME
- APR_HOME used for:
  - Last update check timestamp
  - Downloaded updates cache
  - Any persistent data
```

### 3. stat_portable() - Cross-Platform stat
```bash
# Test cases (CRITICAL for macOS/Linux):
- GNU stat available → uses %Y format
- BSD stat available → uses -f %m format
- Returns modification time as Unix timestamp
- Works on files
- Works on directories
- Handles missing files gracefully
```

### 4. get_terminal_width() - Terminal Size Detection
```bash
# Test cases:
- tput available → uses tput cols
- tput unavailable → falls back to 80
- Respects minimum width
- Respects maximum width
- Non-TTY returns default
```

### 5. get_script_dir() - Script Location
```bash
# Test cases:
- Returns directory containing apr script
- Works when script is symlinked
- Works with absolute path invocation
- Works with relative path invocation
```

### 6. realpath_portable() if it exists
```bash
# Test cases:
- Resolves symlinks
- Works without realpath binary (fallback)
- Handles relative paths
- Handles paths with spaces
```

## Test Implementation
```bash
@test "XDG data home is respected" {
    export XDG_DATA_HOME="/tmp/test_xdg_data"
    mkdir -p "$XDG_DATA_HOME"
    
    source apr
    
    # Trigger something that uses APR_HOME
    # ...
    
    assert [ -d "$XDG_DATA_HOME/apr" ]
}

@test "stat_portable works on Linux and macOS" {
    local test_file=$(mktemp)
    
    source apr
    local mtime=$(stat_portable "$test_file")
    
    # Should be a valid Unix timestamp
    assert [ "$mtime" -gt 1000000000 ]
    
    rm "$test_file"
}
```

## Cross-Platform Testing
- Test on Ubuntu (GNU coreutils)
- Test on macOS (BSD stat)
- Verify no bashisms that break Bash 4.0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] XDG_DATA_HOME respected
- [ ] XDG_CACHE_HOME respected
- [ ] APR_HOME override works
- [ ] stat_portable works on both platforms
- [ ] get_terminal_width handles all cases
- [ ] Path functions work with special characters
- [ ] All tests documented with platform notes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:15:21.725748029-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:15:21.725748029-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-8ij","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:16:36.63519952-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Objective
Test that APR correctly uses XDG Base Directory paths and path utility functions.

## Functions to Test

### 1. XDG Path Configuration
```bash
# APR should use:
# - Data: ${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/apr/
# - Cache: ${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/apr/
# - Config: Per-project .apr/ directory

# Test cases:

- XDG_DATA_HOME unset → uses ~/.local/share/apr/
- XDG_CACHE_HOME set → cache goes there
- XDG_CACHE_HOME unset → uses ~/.cache/apr/

# Directory creation:
- Data directory created if missing
- Cache directory created if missing
- Proper permissions (700 or 755)
```

### 2. APR_HOME Variable
```bash
# Test cases:
- APR_HOME set → overrides default
- APR_HOME unset → uses XDG_DATA_HOME
- APR_HOME used for:
  - Last update check timestamp
  - Downloaded updates cache
  - Any persistent data
```

### 3. stat_portable() - Cross-Platform stat
```bash
# Test cases (CRITICAL for macOS/Linux):
- GNU stat available → uses %Y format
- BSD stat available → uses -f %m format
- Returns modification time as Unix timestamp
- Works on files
- Works on directories
- Handles missing files gracefully
```

### 4. get_terminal_width() - Terminal Size Detection
```bash
# Test cases:
- tput available → uses tput cols
- tput unavailable → falls back to 80
- Respects minimum width
- Respects maximum width
- Non-TTY returns default
```

### 5. get_script_dir() - Script Location
```bash
# Test cases:
- Returns directory containing apr script
- Works when script is symlinked
- Works with absolute path invocation
- Works with relative path invocation
```

### 6. realpath_portable() if it exists
```bash
# Test cases:
- Resolves symlinks
- Works without realpath binary (fallback)
- Handles relative paths
- Handles paths with spaces
```

## Test Implementation
```bash
@test "XDG data home is respected" {
    export XDG_DATA_HOME="/tmp/test_xdg_data"
    mkdir -p "$XDG_DATA_HOME"
    
    source apr
    
    # Trigger something that uses APR_HOME
    # ...

}

@test "stat_portable works on Linux and macOS" {
    local test_file=$(mktemp)
    
    source apr
    local mtime=$(stat_portable "$test_file")
    
    # Should be a valid Unix timestamp
    assert [ "$mtime" -gt 1000000000 ]
    
    rm "$test_file"
}
```

## Cross-Platform Testing
- Test on Ubuntu (GNU coreutils)
- Test on macOS (BSD stat)
- Verify no bashisms that break Bash 4.0

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] XDG_DATA_HOME respected
- [ ] XDG_CACHE_HOME respected
- [ ] APR_HOME override works
- [ ] stat_portable works on both platforms
- [ ] get_terminal_width handles all cases
- [ ] Path functions work with special characters

 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ayr","title":"Unit Tests: Utility Functions (version_gt, can_prompt, iso_timestamp)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Utility Functions

## Objective
Create comprehensive unit tests for all utility functions in APR without using mocks.

## Functions to Test

### 1. version_gt() - Semantic Version Comparison
Test cases:
- version_gt 1.2.0 1.1.0 = true (minor bump)
- version_gt 2.0.0 1.9.9 = true (major bump)
- version_gt 1.0.1 1.0.0 = true (patch bump)
- version_gt 1.0.0 1.0.0 = false (equal)
- version_gt 1.0.0 1.0.1 = false (older)
- version_gt 1.10.0 1.9.0 = true (double digit)
- version_gt 0.1.0 0.0.9 = true (zero major)
Edge cases:
- Empty strings
- Invalid version formats
- Sort -V fallback vs string comparison

### 2. can_prompt() - Interactive Terminal Detection
Test cases:
- TTY on stdin and stderr = true
- No TTY on stdin = false
- No TTY on stderr = false
- QUIET_MODE=true = false
Real testing approach:
- Use script(1) or expect to simulate TTY
- Test with actual pipes/redirects

### 3. iso_timestamp() - ISO8601 Timestamp
Test cases:
- Format matches YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ
- Timezone is UTC (Z suffix)
- Incrementing calls show time progression

### 4. verbose() - Debug Logging
Test cases:
- VERBOSE=false = no output
- VERBOSE=true = output to stderr
- Output format includes [apr:verbose] prefix

### 5. check_gum() - Gum Availability (NEW)
Test cases:
- gum in PATH = GUM_AVAILABLE=true
- gum not in PATH = GUM_AVAILABLE=false
- APR_NO_GUM=1 = GUM_AVAILABLE=false even if gum exists
- CI=true = gum suppressed
- GITHUB_ACTIONS=true = gum suppressed

### 6. check_oracle() - Oracle Availability (NEW)
Test cases:
- oracle command in PATH = returns 0, method=global
- oracle not in PATH but npx available = returns 0, method=npx
- Neither available = returns 1
- Sets ORACLE_CMD correctly

### 7. try_install_gum() - Gum Installation (NEW)
Test cases:
- Package manager detection (brew, apt, etc.)
- Interactive prompt for installation
- Non-interactive mode skips
- Installation failure handling

## Test File: tests/unit/test_utils.bats

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
- Function name being tested
- Input values
- Expected output
- Actual output
- Pass/fail with reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] version_gt: 10+ test cases covering all scenarios
- [ ] can_prompt: 5+ test cases with real TTY simulation
- [ ] iso_timestamp: Format validation tests
- [ ] verbose: Output control tests
- [ ] check_gum: Environment variable behavior
- [ ] check_oracle: Both global and npx paths
- [ ] try_install_gum: Installation flow
- [ ] All tests log detailed information
- [ ] No mocks - real function behavior only","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:07:48.13409656-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T20:59:53.6584292-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T20:59:53.6584292-05:00","close_reason":"Comprehensive unit tests already implemented in tests/unit/. test_utils.bats covers version_gt (8 tests), iso_timestamp (3 tests), verbose (3 tests), can_prompt (2 tests), check_gum (3 tests). test_config.bats covers get_config_value (6 tests), get_yaml_block (5 tests), load_prompt_template (4 tests), ensure_config_dir (3 tests), load_config (2 tests). test_lock.bats covers acquire_lock (7 tests), release_lock (3 tests), cleanup_temp (4 tests). All 78 tests passing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-ayr","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:12:37.2332782-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-de5","title":"Unit Tests: Robot Mode JSON Functions (robot_json, robot_status, robot_workflows, etc.)","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Robot Mode Functions

## Objective
Test all robot mode functions that produce JSON output.

## Functions to Test

### 1. robot_json() - JSON Response Builder
```bash
# Test cases:
- ok=true, code="ok" → valid JSON envelope
- ok=false, code="error" → valid JSON envelope
- hint parameter included when provided
- hint parameter omitted when empty
- meta.v contains VERSION
- meta.ts is valid ISO8601
- ROBOT_COMPACT=true → minified output
- ROBOT_COMPACT=false → pretty-printed

# JSON validation:
- All outputs parseable by jq
- No trailing characters
- Correct escaping of special characters in data
```

### 2. robot_status() - System Status
```bash
# Test cases:
- Not configured → configured:false
- Configured → configured:true, default_workflow set
- Workflows listed in array
- Oracle available via global → method:"global"
- Oracle available via npx → method:"npx"
- Oracle not available → oracle_available:false
- Hint provided when not configured
- Hint provided when oracle missing
```

### 3. robot_workflows() - Workflow Listing
```bash
# Test cases:
- No workflows dir → not_configured error
- Empty workflows dir → empty array
- Multiple workflows → array with name/description
- Description extraction from YAML
```

### 4. robot_init() - Initialization
```bash
# Test cases:
- Already initialized → created:false, existed:true
- Fresh init → created:true, existed:false
- Directory creation failure → init_failed error
- Config write failure → init_failed error
```

### 5. robot_validate() - Pre-run Validation
```bash
# Test cases:
- Valid state → valid:true, empty errors
- Round number missing → errors array
- Workflow missing → errors array
- Files missing → errors array
- Previous round missing → warnings array (not errors)
- JSON structure correct for all cases
```

### 6. robot_run() - Execution
```bash
# Test cases (dry-run/validation only, not actual Oracle):
- Missing round → missing_argument error
- Non-numeric round → invalid_argument error
- Workflow not found → not_found error
- Files not found → not_found error
- Oracle not available → dependency_missing error
- Valid request → returns slug, pid, output_file

# Note: Actual Oracle execution tested in integration
```

### 7. robot_history() - Round Listing
```bash
# Test cases:
- No rounds dir → not_found error
- Empty rounds dir → empty rounds array
- Multiple rounds → array with round, file, size, modified
- JSON numbers (not strings) for round, size, modified
- tonumber conversion handles edge cases
```

### 8. robot_help() - Help Output
```bash
# Test cases:
- Valid JSON structure
- All commands documented
- All options documented
- Examples included
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All robot_* functions produce valid JSON
- [ ] Error responses use correct codes
- [ ] Hints are helpful for error resolution
- [ ] jq can parse all outputs
- [ ] ROBOT_COMPACT mode works
- [ ] No bash errors leak into JSON output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:09:35.23404558-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T21:42:53.734327441-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T21:42:53.734327441-05:00","close_reason":"Added unit tests for robot mode JSON output","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-de5","depends_on_id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-hqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T20:12:37.376856509-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"automated_plan_reviser_pro-fgx","title":"Unit Tests: Exit Codes and Error Handling","description":"# Task: Unit Tests for Exit Codes and Error Handling

## Objective
Verify all EXIT_* constants are used correctly and error handling is consistent.

## Exit Codes to Test

From apr script - verify these constants exist and are used correctly:
- EXIT_SUCCESS=0
- EXIT_ERROR=1
- EXIT_USAGE=2
- EXIT_CONFIG_ERROR=3
- EXIT_NETWORK_ERROR=4
- EXIT_UPDATE_ERROR=5
- EXIT_ORACLE_ERROR=6
- EXIT_LOCK_ERROR=7

## Test Scenarios

### 1. EXIT_SUCCESS (0)
Commands that should return 0:
- apr --help
- apr --version
- apr list (with workflows)
- apr history (with rounds)
- apr show N (existing round)
- apr run N --dry-run (valid config)
- apr setup (completed successfully)
- apr robot status (valid response)
- apr robot help

### 2. EXIT_ERROR (1) - General Error
- Invalid command: apr invalidcmd
- File not found during operation
- General runtime errors

### 3. EXIT_USAGE (2) - Usage Error
- Missing required argument: apr run (no round number)
- Invalid option: apr --invalid-option
- apr robot run (missing round)
- apr show (missing round number)

### 4. EXIT_CONFIG_ERROR (3)
- apr run 1 (no .apr directory)
- apr run 1 -w nonexistent (workflow not found)
- Malformed workflow YAML
- Missing required config keys

### 5. EXIT_NETWORK_ERROR (4)
- apr update (network timeout)
- Update check with unreachable server

### 6. EXIT_UPDATE_ERROR (5)
- Downloaded file has wrong shebang
- Checksum mismatch
- Update file not writable

### 7. EXIT_ORACLE_ERROR (6)
- Oracle not available (neither global nor npx)
- Oracle command fails
- apr run with Oracle returning error

### 8. EXIT_LOCK_ERROR (7)
- Another apr instance running (concurrent lock)
- Lock file in use

## Robot Mode Error Codes
Robot mode should return ok:false with specific codes:
- missing_argument: when required arg missing
- invalid_argument: when arg format wrong
- not_configured: when .apr not initialized
- not_found: when workflow/round missing
- dependency_missing: when Oracle not available
- init_failed: when initialization fails
- unknown_command: for unrecognized robot commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All EXIT_* constants documented
- [ ] Each exit code tested with specific trigger
- [ ] Error messages are helpful
- [ ] Robot mode error codes all verified
- [ ] No exit code collisions
- [ ] set -e behavior verified (errors exit)
